seoTitle: How to Change Status Colors in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to edit status colors in ClickUp to match your team's branding, using either the default palette or custom hex codes.
intro: Status colors are one of the fastest visual cues on a Kanban board, so getting them consistent with your team's branding or mental model makes lists easier to scan. This guide covers finding the status color settings inside a Space, choosing colors from the default palette or a custom hex code, and applying overrides at the List level when needed.

steps.heading: Open Space Settings
steps.body: Hover over a target Space, click the three-dot settings icon, and select Space Settings.

steps.heading: Go to Task Statuses
steps.body: Open the Task Statuses panel to view your current pipeline and its color assignments.

steps.heading: Click the color circle next to a status
steps.body: Select the small color circle beside an existing status name to open the color picker.

steps.heading: Choose a color or enter a hex code
steps.body: Pick a color from the pop-up palette, or type a custom brand hex code for exact color matching.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Click Save Changes to push the updated colors across the entire Space.

steps.heading: Apply a List-level override if needed
steps.body: Navigate into a specific List to run a granular status color customization separate from the parent Space.

faq.question: Can I use my company's exact brand colors for statuses?
faq.answer: Yes, you can input a custom hex code when editing a status color to match your brand palette exactly.

faq.question: Does changing a status color affect existing tasks in that status?
faq.answer: No, changing the color only updates how the status displays visually; the tasks assigned to it stay exactly where they are.

faq.question: Can different Lists in the same Space have different status colors?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply a List-level override to customize colors separately from the Space's default settings.

faq.question: Where will the updated colors show up?
faq.answer: Updated status colors appear across Kanban board, List, and Table views wherever that status is displayed.
